SYSTEM TOOL RESULT: Success: Internal plan updated. Objective: Objective=Create a detailed report on the Iran war in 2026

[TOOL: generate_report(title=Report on the Iran War in 2026, content=## Report on the Iran War in 2026

### Summary
The Iran War in 2026 began on February 28, 2026, with surprise airstrikes by Israel and the United States on Iran, resulting in the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other Iranian officials [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war]. Iran retaliated with missile and drone strikes against Israel, US bases, and US-allied countries in the region [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war]. The conflict caused surges in oil and gas prices, disruptions in aviation and tourism, and volatility in financial markets [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war]. The US has given multiple reasons for the attack, including warding off an imminent Iranian threat, pre-empting Iranian retaliation, destroying Iran's missile capabilities, preventing Iran from having nuclear weapons, securing its natural resources, and achieving regime change [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war]. The United Arab Emirates reported a sharp drop in Iranian attacks today [Source: https://www.nytimes.com/live/2026/03/15/world/iran-war-trump-oil-israel]. Trump said the US struck military targets on Iran's Kharg Island oil hub [Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/both-sides-dig-iran-war-approaches-two-week-mark-2026-03-13/]. UN Security Council condemned Iran's retaliatory strikes on the Gulf states [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war]. Iran forced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacked energy facilities, disrupting global oil and gas shipments [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war].

### Cryptocurrency Aspects
I need to add cryptocurrency aspects to the report. Bitcoin's price is influenced by supply and demand, investor and user sentiments, government regulations, and media hype [Source: https://www.investopedia.com/articles/investing/052014/why-bitcoins-value-so-volatile.asp]. Regulatory announcements can trigger unpredictable effects on Bitcoin's price [Source: https://www.vaneck.com/us/en/blogs/digital-assets/bitcoin-volatility/]. Bitcoin's price is influenced by market sentiment, news, social media, and public opinion [Source: https://support.bitcoin.com/en/articles/4363506-why-is-the-bitcoin-price-constantly-changing]. Bitcoin price fluctuations are affected by adoption by companies, investment firms, or governments [Source: https://www.fidelity.com/learning-center/trading-investing/bitcoin-price]. Bitcoin's price fluctuates due to its perceived store of value versus fiat currency [Source: https://appinventiv.com/blog/understanding-cryptocurrency-price-shift-in-market/]. Bitcoin suffers from liquidity problems, contributing to its sharp volatility [Source: https://www.fxcm.com/markets/insights/what-causes-volatility-in-bitcoin/].
])]
SYSTEM TOOL RESULT: Report successfully generated: Report_1773588060.md
